body.title: Move Claude Code crash-recovery call from bash into Igor exception handler
body.result: Main loop except handler now calls 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions' with the full Python traceback. Skips CC if debug_session.flag is present. Exits with code 42 (restart signal) so bash loop restarts cleanly without double-invoking CC. Bash remains as fallback for startup/import crashes Python can't catch.
body.description: Currently the igor bash startup script detects crashes and calls Claude Code externally. With T-main-loop-exception adding an outer try-except in main.py, Igor can invoke Claude Code from inside Python on unhandled exceptions — much cleaner, gets full Python traceback context, can pass structured crash info. Fix: in the outer except handler, call subprocess to launch CC with the crash details passed as structured args. This removes the crash-detection logic from bash entirely and makes Igor self-aware of his own crashes. Akien's suggestion from 2026-04-04.
